channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Does Crimson Desert Have Difficulty Settings?
item.content:encoded:
If you’re here, you’re probably finding Crimson Desert a tad bit difficult, but fear not, you’re not alone. Plenty of encounters are on the challenging side of things, especially with the variety of bosses and skills to master.

That poses the question, though: does Crimson Desert have difficulty settings? It’s a valid quandary, especially with the modern day of countless accessibility settings to make gaming as accessible for as many as possible.

That being said, some titles are simply meant to be more difficult than others, but is Crimson Desert one of them? Insider Gaming has all the answers you could need below.

Can You Change The Difficulty In Crimson Desert?

It’s bad news for those of you struggling out there, as there are no adjustable difficulty settings in Crimson Desert.

It seems like Pearl Abyss just wants to see you suffer, as there’s no way to make the game easier for yourself in the settings. Instead, you’ll have to buckle down and work hard to get past any boss you’re struggling with, as they’ll keep dishing out the damage whether you like it or not.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Clear The Fog Of War In Crimson Desert

However, it’s not all bad news. Sure, you can’t adjust the difficulty in the settings, but there are some ways to make the game easier for yourself, and Insider Gaming has some suggestions below.

How To Make Crimson Desert Easier

The main piece of advice to make Crimson Desert easier is to take your time with the campaign, as there are numerous spikes in difficulty that may catch you off guard.

A handful of bosses arrive as a skill check of sorts, as they pair Kliff up against a tough opponent that can be incredibly tough, even for the most skilled of gamers out there. That’s why it’s important to make sure Kliff is appropriately levelled up for each encounter, which you can manage by completing a variety of side content.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Increase Inventory Size In Crimson Desert

Additionally, make sure you’re stocked up on food, as that’s your main source of healing. Don’t enter a boss fight without a healthy collection of recipes, and make sure they heal enough health at once.

Finally, get out there and explore. You’d be surprised how much gear is hidden in ruins and behind puzzles out in the world of Pywel. Try new weapon types, experiment with different Abyss Cores, and you’ll find yourself getting stronger and stronger. Before you know it, it’s time to take on the boss who gave you grief and show them who’s in charge.

How are you finding Crimson Desert? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Crimson Desert content, check out the following guides:

  • How To Open Locked Doors In Crimson Desert
  • How To Steal Items In Crimson Desert
  • How To Unlock Double Jump In Crimson Desert

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Can You Play Crimson Desert In First Person?
item.content:encoded:
Most games tend to stick to one perspective, but it’s becoming increasingly common for some to offer the chance to switch between first and third person. Each has its own advantages, but some people undoubtedly prefer one over the other.

Crimson Desert has been primarily marketed as a third-person RPG, which might leave some fans of the FPS genre a bit left out. However, if you’re interested in exploring the expansive world of Pywel in first person, then you might just be in luck.

Here’s whether you can play Crimson Desert in first person.

Can You Play Crimson Desert In First Person?

FPS fans, rejoice! You can absolutely play Crimson Desert in first person, and it’s incredibly seamless to switch.

All you need to do is toggle the distance that the camera is away from the protagonist. As if one third-person perspective wasn’t enough, Crimson Desert offers various distances from the player, with both a more zoomed out, detached experience, as well as the closer, more personal one.

  • More Insider Gaming: Does Crimson Desert Have Difficulty Settings?

But if you’re looking to really immerse yourself in the world of Pywel, just toggle the camera once more to see through the eyes of the protagonist. Stare out across the beautiful landscapes or explore the tight quarters of the wonderfully designed interiors. It’s an FPS dream, but it does unfortunately come with a major caveat…

Can You Play Crimson Desert Solely In First Person?

The dream won’t last forever, as you cannot play through the entirety of Crimson Desert in first person.

It’s a tough pill to swallow, FPS fans, but whenever you draw your weapon, you’ll exit first-person mode and be returned to third person instead. That means whenever combat arises, which is fairly regularly in this Action-RPG, you’ll be flushed out of the character’s eyes and thrown into the heat of battle.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Increase Inventory Size In Crimson Desert

It’s a shame, as the world of Crimson Desert looks even more stunning in first person, but the gameplay has to come first, and that was primarily designed with third person in mind. Who knows, though, perhaps in a future update, you’ll be able to play through the entire game in first person, but for now, you’ll have to keep your eyes in the sky.

How are you finding Crimson Desert? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Crimson Desert content, check out the following guides:

  • How To Clear The Fog Of War In Crimson Desert
  • How To Steal Items In Crimson Desert
  • How To Unlock Double Jump In Crimson Desert
